46.

48.
49.
50. Surprise cat hopes you have an awesome day!
item.dc:creator: Scrown0

item.title: Repost but hope seems apt for today
item.description:
Most viral edit: Thanks for the awards and upvotes y'all! Just like the author of the post, I didn't worry too much about the technical details or facts. I think of the Mr Rogers quote as my main takeaway: "My mother would say to me, 'Look for the helpers. You will always find people who are helping. 'To this day, especially in times of disaster, I remember my mother's words, and I am always comforted by realizing that there are still so many helpers — so many caring people in this world." Original post below This is saved in my favorites and I refer to it at least once a month, maybe more. I have a feeling I am going to lean on this a lot more for the next few years. There are times when I will get overwhelmed and want to walk away from everything, I'm hoping a part of me remembers to come back to this post and not do that. I have the text memorized and I could write down most of it if I lost this post. But I am grateful to the original poster and wanted to make sure to give them the credit they deserve: https://imgur.com/gallery/PYYeTxm
Dog tax: it took Charlie 4 months to trust me enough to do this.
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: God forbid they monitor the people who steal billions. Or murderers.

item.description: Unfortunately I don't think my landlord accepts smiles as payment. Guess I'll try again next month.

item.title: Imagine that (yourself)
item.description: Found in the wild and it made me laugh.
item.dc:creator: UncleMartha

item.title: I eat my veggies
item.description: I was at the grocery store, walked up to the zucchini pile and this old lady walks up as I picked up one. She then releases a shrill raspy accusation that I’ve taken the one and only zucchini she wanted from the pile and needs it! I was sorta stunned at her arrogance and entitlement. All I could say was ‘Thank you for allowing me to ruin your day’ and I walked away. Old people can be the worst.

item.title: Squirrel Girl!
item.description:
MY FRIENDS ARE HERE!!!! Is anyone else playing Marvel Rivals a lot lately? I’ve been sick for almost a couple weeks now and haven’t left my place much, so when I’m not working I’ve just been playing it a lot. I LOVE playing as : Cloak and Dagger and the Invisible Woman. I wanted to draw Squirrel Girl cuz her design is just soooo cute. I’m branching out to more fanart, so if you’re interesting in seeing more of that or have any requests, feel free to comment. Alternate version: www.patreon.com/miaormoa
MY FRIENDS ARE HERE!!!! Is anyone else playing Marvel Rivals a lot lately? I’ve been sick for almost a couple weeks now and haven’t left my place much, so when I’m not working I’ve just been playing it a lot. I LOVE playing as : Cloak and Dagger and the Invisible Woman. I wanted to draw Squirrel Girl cuz her design is just soooo cute. I’m branching out to more fanart, so if you’re interesting in seeing more of that or have any requests, feel free to comment. Alternate version: www.patreon.com/miaormoa
item.dc:creator: miaormoa

item.title: Be the thorn.
item.description: Remember, You don't know any trans people, You don't know any gay people, You don't know any immigrants, You don't know anyone who is pregnant, You don't know anyone who has had a abortion, You don't know any who is Jewish, You don't know anyone who is Muslim, You don't know anyone who is atheist, You didn't see anything, You didn't hear anything, You don't know anything, A cop asks you a question You say I don't speak to Police, They try and stop you, You ask am I being detained? If they say no, walk away. If they say yes, say I will not speak with out a lawyer, I request a lawyer. Say it exactly like that. The police and the government are a hostile force and cannot be trusted. If you can wipe everything off FB, twitter, Instagram and email their support teams and tell them to delete your data from their systems. Every election you register for, sheriff, mayor, governor, no matter how small. Register and vote. Our job for the next 4 years is to be the biggest legal thorn in their side. Flood your rep with emails, letters and calls. Be the thorn.
item.dc:creator: thetinymonarch

item.dc:creator: theshopliftingseagull

item.title: I lived, bitch!
item.description: I’ve been home for about a week now, but I actually was in the hospital from Christmas Day until last Sunday. Christmas Eve I couldn’t sleep. I kept getting sick and was just feeling off. My wife woke up Christmas Day and I told her “Something is wrong”. I barely remember the kids opening gifts. After that my memory is non existent. I had hallucinations for days. The first thing I remember is waking up after surgery. I a tube up nose into my stomach and I had an intestinal blockage that had to be removed. I hadn’t eaten anything in 14 days by the time the tube was taken out. I lost around 30lbs. Now I had to try to start walking. Painful with a several inch cut in my gut. It was a close call. I was apparently moved to another hospital. I didn’t even remover being transferred. It’s weird just missing that time. But I made it. I’m alive. My wife was my advocate and fought to save me (first hospital wanted to send me home saying it would pass on its own). Just glad to be among you degenerates again.
item.dc:creator: OnyxPrimal

item.title: The Wood-maiden of Gondor
item.description:
This is a lore account on Vidumavi, princess from the North. She married a future king of Gondor and endured hatred for her kin (photo: Verbena's Dream Studio).
In the 13th century of the Third Age, about 1800 years before The Lord of the Rings, Gondor was vast and mighty but always needed more soldiers (art by Jon Bosco).
While the lords and much of the population in the hearthland were Dúnedain, many army recruits were Northmen: a kindred of peoples that included the forefathers of the Rohirrim, Dale-men, and Beornings (art by Johnny Shumate).
The region of Rhovanion lay between the Misty Mountains and Rhûn, and Northmen lived across Rhovanion. The Northmen on the plains were ruled by princes [Tolkien used prince in its original meang of a high noble, like a duke, not for son of a king].
One Prince was named Vidugavia, and despite a limited domain Vidugavia "called himself King of Rhovanion, and was indeed the most powerful of the Northern princes" (The Return of the King; Apppendix A; I; IV onward, art by Tomas Duchek).
In the 13th century many Northmen lived under the rule of Gondor (estimated territory in blue) or as tributaries (estimated in red). The land of Vidugavia (estimated in green) lay between the Greenwood [Mirkwood] and the river Celduin; if Lake-town existed at this time it may been within his realm.
The kings of Gondor intended for the Northmen on the plains to be a buffer against invasions by Easterlings (art by Angus McBride). However "the Northmen did not always remain true to Gondor, and some would join forces with the Easterlings, either out of greed for spoil, or in the furtherance of feuds among their princes."
Yet in 1248 the ruling king of Gondor broke an army of Easterlings, and he was second to take the name Rómendacil [East-victor]. Note: in Dúnedain names is C pronounced as K. Thereafter King Rómendacil II let build the great statues of the Argonath, as a warning to the Northmen to not threaten Gondor (art by Armand Cabrera).
"But since he needed men, and desired to strengthen the bond between Gondor and the Northmen, he took many of them into his service and gave to some high rank in his armies. Rómendacil showed especial favour to Vidugavia, who had aided him in the war" (art by Tom Romain).
In 1250 Rómendacil sent his heir Valacar, age 56, to Vidugavia as an ambassador. It would allow Valacar to learn the language, manners, and policies of the Northmen; and to dissuade them from turning on Gondor (art by Catherine Karina Chmiel).
"But Valacar far exceeded his father's designs. He grew to love the Northern lands and people, and he married Vidumavi, daughter of Vidugavia." From this marriage came later the war of the Kin-strife, but it seems like Rómendacil accepted his daugher-in-law due to politics (photo: Verbena's Dream Studio).
"Romendakil gave his consent to the marriage. He could not forbid it or refuse to recognize it without earning the enmity of Vidugavia. Indeed all the Northmen would have been angered, and those in his service would have been no longer to be trusted." – The History of Middle-Earth 12; I; IX (art by JB Casacop)
Valacar and Vidumavi had at least two sons but only Vinitharya, the oldest son, is known to us. Vinitharya was born in 1255 and named in the local speech. These Northmen names are Gothic in form, to represent them being far older than the names encountered in the later Rohan and Dale, where names are given in the related but comparably younger Old English (photo: Verbena's Dream Studio).
Vidugavi is a latinized form of Gothic Widugauja, meaning "Wood-dweller", while Vidumavi means "Wood-maiden" (Unfinished Tales; III; II; Note 6). Vinitharya translates into "Wends-army man" (elfdict.com), to be understood as East-victor (HoME 12; I; IX + RotK; Appendix A; I; IV), clearly to honour his grandfather.
In 1260 King Rómendacil "recalled Valakar, saying that it was now time that he took part in the councils of the realm and the command of its armies. Valakar returned to Gondor with his wife and children; and with them came a household of noble men and women of the North" (HoME 12; I; IX, photo: Verbena's Dream Studio).
The Dúnedain spoke the Common Speech as well as Elven Sindarin, and the kings took names in the High Elf language of Quenya. Therefore Valacar gave Vinitharya the name Eldacar (Q. Elfhelm) for public use in Gondor. Meanwhile "his wife bore herself wisely and endeared herself to all those who knew her."
"She learned well the speech and manners of Gondor, and was willing to be called by the name Galadwen, a rendering of her Northern name into the Sindarin tongue. She was a fair and noble lady of high courage, which she imparted to her children;" Despite this hate grew against herself and her children, though Galadwen seemingly did not dignify it with attention (art by PrincepsSenatus).
This malice came from proud and racist nobles, many of the royal line, and their followers who looked down on the short-lived Men of Middle-earth (RotK; Appendix A; I; IV): "For the high men of Gondor already looked askance at the Northmen among them; and it was a thing unheard of before that the heir to the crown, or any son of the King, should wed one of lesser and alien race."
Vidumavi "lived to a great age, as such was reckoned among her people" but could not compare to the long-lived Dúnedain (HoME 12; I; IX, original art by SYoshiko). She may have passed away in 1344, but more likley in 1332; the latter means she would been over 77 years old but if the former she was over 100. Though she is often thought of as a Queen she had been dead for decades once Valacar became King in 1366.
Valacar ruled until 1432, and passed away at age 238. The Kin-strife, the bloody civil war that was one of three great disasters which severly drained the strength of Gondor, erupted as soon as Eldacar inherited the throne of the kingdom. In 1437 he was defeated but eluded his foes and "came to the North, to his kinsfolk in Rhovanion" (RotK; Appendix A; I; IV, art by Michael Merulla).
"Many gathered to him there, both of the Northmen in the service of Gondor, and of the Dúnedain of the northern parts of the realm. For many of the latter had learned to esteem him, and many more came to hate his usurper." –ibid Those who had foretold the heritage of Vidumavi to be detrimental would see Eldacar reclaim his throne in 1447 and rule until 1490, at age 235 (art by MatejCadil).
It is possible however that her Northmen heritage provided one or more kings of Gondor with beard. Tolkien came to conclude that beardlessness was common among humans with an Elven heritage (UT; II; IV), and finally that it was a core feature of Elven ancestry until the LotR (The Nature of Middle-earth; II; V). However, Frodo and Samwise saw that a king of Gondor had been portrayed with a beard (The Two Towers; IV; VII); if to reconcile this Galadwen may be a solution.
In 2510, ca 500 years before the LotR, a folk of Northmen rode to the aid of Gondor and were rewarded with land. This became Rohan, and the kings of Rohan descended from Vidugavia (Unfinished Tales; III; II; Note 6); ie, a sibling of Vidumavi. Therefore, despite that the tie was very ancient, the kings of Rohan "accounted themselves kinsmen of the kings of Gondor descended from Eldacar" (RotK; Appendix A; II); these would include Aragorn, though in his case it was in a broken line.
Some related accounts (art by Cristian Otazu): The Northmen https://imgur.com/gallery/ot08BD9 The usurper of Gondor https://imgur.com/gallery/LpnDHHu The Fallen King at the Cross-roads https://imgur.com/gallery/CUzUrku
In case you enjoyed this and would like to read further Tolkien lore posts about characters, themes, objects and events, here are my indexes: Character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF Folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: Practicing a new art style!
item.description:
I’ve been playing Nine Sols recently (amazing game, you should 100% play it if you haven’t already) and its art style has really inspired me! I’ve been doing a lot of studies based off screenshots of the game. I’m really happy with how these came out and have learned so much from these exercises. I’m planning on continuing practicing this style and getting the hang of it. Hop you enjoy them as much as I enjoyed making them.
Viral edit: OMG, you dirty birds!!! I did NOT draw any of this with erotic themes…! I swear, I was using references!!! It’s pretty nature stuff! From a metroidvania!!! Y’all need fresh air and to cool off….but thank you for the kind words, you dirty animals. ;)
item.dc:creator: TheCrookedCrow
